description: The 4-step order (comment → squash → branch-delete → issue-close) is the whole point of the skill — it must be stated as load-bearing so a future editor doesn't 'optimize' it into a different order.
Must document WHY squash precedes branch-delete (so the squash commit still references the branch's work) — the rationale is what stops the order from being reshuffled.
The defining brain-os nuance: code lives in the code repo, issues in the tracker repo, so the close reference needs the explicit repo prefix. Without this the issue silently never closes.
Must spell out the failure mode — a bare `Closes #N` targets the code repo and no-ops — so the cross-repo rule reads as a real bug-prevention, not pedantry.
expect: a bare `Closes #N` targets the code repo and no-ops

description: Issue close must route through the central close-issue.sh helper (single close site) — it strips ghost status:* labels and is idempotent on already-closed issues.
Must explicitly forbid inlining `gh issue close` — inlining re-leaks the ghost status:* labels the helper exists to strip (the original bug from impl #160).
The skill must point at the real helper path so step 4 is executable, not a vague instruction.
Squash subject must be the PR title verbatim (pass --subject) — the original feedback rule. Without --subject, gh appends ` (#N)` and the subject drifts.
expect: PR title verbatim as the squash subject

description: Merging is the one irreversible remote action — the skill must preview the plan before it, matching the global 'confirm destructive remote actions first' rule (with a pre-authorized fast path).
A silent half-merge (merged but branch/issue left dangling) is the exact drift this skill prevents — so it must verify each step and fail loud rather than log pass on a partial run.
The close-without-merge (abandon) path must leave linked issues OPEN — the work isn't done — so it must NOT call close-issue.sh. Distinguishes 'land' from 'reject'.
Must resolve the tracker repo from gh_task_repo config so the cross-repo close reference is configurable, not hardcoded per user.

Skill must call scripts/pr-close.ts prepare to build the cross-repo close block + verbatim subject — the load-bearing strings are unit-tested there (pr-close.test.ts), not improvised in prose where a bare #N would silently no-op.
